# Vakeel Street

> Indian legal document drafting and advisory for Non-Resident Indians worldwide. Every document is drafted personally by Xitij Kaushik — an advocate enrolled at the High Court of Uttarakhand, India (Enrollment No. UK/429/13), based in Dubai. No office visit required. 48-hour delivery.

Vakeel Street exists for one reason: NRIs need Indian legal documents drafted to a standard that holds — in court, before a government authority, or in a property transaction — and they need an advocate who will actually respond. The service is delivered entirely by email. The advocate reads your situation, drafts the document, and delivers it within 48 hours.

The service covers NRIs in all countries, for legal matters across all Indian states. It does not cover court appearances or representation before Indian authorities (UAE law governs where Vakeel Street operates from). It covers drafting and advisory — the document layer that moves everything else forward.

## Services

- [Home — How it works](https://vakeelstreet.com): Three-step process. You share documents and situation; Xitij drafts; you move the matter forward in India through a POA holder, relative, or authorised party.
- [Proof of work](https://vakeelstreet.com/proof.html): Published briefs demonstrating drafting approach and analytical depth.
- [Terms of service](https://vakeelstreet.com/tos.html): Scope of service, UAE non-practice declaration, document-based advice only, no retainer.

## What gets drafted

Complaints — consumer court, criminal, civil, against government authorities (IG, RPO, District Magistrate, Collector).
Writ petitions — High Court standard.
RTI applications — Right to Information, all Indian states and central government authorities.
Powers of Attorney — property transactions, court matters, administrative filings; drafted for NRI execution before Indian Consulate or Notary abroad.
Contracts — sale agreements, leave and licence, any bilateral instrument.
Legal representations — to ministries, departments, regulatory bodies.
Strategic briefs — analysis of legal position, recommended course of action, document sequencing for complex matters.

## Who drafts

Xitij Kaushik. Advocate. Enrollment No. UK/429/13. High Court of Uttarakhand. Based in Dubai, UAE. He reads every query, responds personally, and drafts every document himself. No junior counsel. No outsourcing. No templates.

## Contact

vakeelstreet@gmail.com

## Published writing

https://medium.com/@vakeelstreet — seven articles on Indian legal matters affecting NRIs: property disputes, RTI, the first step toward legal clarity.
